detail: shipped MyOrchestrator skeleton: fleet-wide next-work picker, deterministic ranking + one optional tie-break Engine call, green ruff+pytest locally

dependency graph among unbuilt tools lives in a hand-maintained manifest.json (not YAML) — runtime must stay dependency-free, so stdlib json over a third-party YAML parser; canonical copy belongs in mythings-core/docs/tools/ as a follow-up, shipped here as package data for now
scope is fleet-wide by default (reads every repo under the MyThingsLab org), not an opt-in --repos flag — the fleet view is the tool's entire purpose; confirmed the doc's open question

the single pinned 'next up' tracking issue lives in the existing mythings-core repo, not a dedicated ops repo — premature to spin up an ops repo at 2-3 repos; configurable via --tracking-repo/--tracking-issue
a built tool's backlog label defaults to its repo name (matches my-guard's CLAUDE.md label 'my-guard'); overridable later if the convention diverges

repo listing and issue createdAt read via the injected gh Runner directly, not github.GitHub — repo_list is in the pending core-additions batch and BUILD_GUIDE forbids adding core methods reactively mid-tool; interim keeps the gh process the only mocked boundary
CI was red only because mythings-core's main was stale (design/next-tools + MBAI-naming fix hadn't merged yet); now merged, re-ran the same commit's CI and it passed, no code change needed
